privileged_info_summary: The task uses real academic papers, not synthetic data. The task sources papers from two paper lists: CLASSIC_PAPERS (10 papers) and ML_PAPERS (8 papers). When run in 'all' mode, it seeds the Zotero library with both lists. There are exactly 3 papers published in Nature: Watson & Crick's 1953 DNA structure paper, and two modern ML papers (LeCun et al. 2015 on Deep Learning, Silver et al. 2016 on AlphaGo). There are exactly 4 papers published in Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS): Vaswani et al. 2017 (Transformer/Attention), Krizhevsky et al. 2012 (AlexNet/ImageNet), Goodfellow et al. 2014 (GANs), and Brown et al. 2020 (GPT-3). All paper titles and publication venues match the metadata claims exactly.
